title: MisconductComplaint
description: A newly filed complaint of NYPD misconduct against an officer — the intake (write) surface. NET-NEW: today a member of the public can file only through the online web form (nyc.gov/site/ccrb .../file-online), by phone (311), by mail, or in person; there is NO machine-readable intake API. This schema serves the write path (file a complaint) and the resulting read of a submission's status, which today is checked through the Complaint Status Lookup app (apps.nyc.gov/ccrb-status-lookup). It is the intake counterpart to the closed-case Complaint object.

trackingNumber.description: Server-assigned complaint tracking number. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Intake/processing status. Set by CCRB, not the filer.

allegationSummary.description: Free-text description of what the officer did — the substance of the misconduct allegation.

incidentAddress.description: Street address or intersection where the incident occurred.

accusedOfficers.description: One or more accused officers, as much as the complainant can identify (shield/name/command/description). CCRB identifies officers where possible; unidentified officers are allowed.

officerDescription.description: Physical or other description of the officer(s) when shield/name is unknown.

videoAvailable.description: Whether the complainant has video (e.g. phone footage) of the incident.

witnesses.description: Names or descriptions of any witnesses.

complainant.description: Filer contact details. A complaint may be filed anonymously, but CCRB cannot follow up without contact information.
